evals.description: Trigger test: activates on schema design question
evals.prompt: I'm building an e-commerce platform and need to model products, orders, and customers. Should I store the product price on the order line item or just reference the product table?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on slow query / performance question
evals.prompt: Our orders table has 50 million rows and a query filtering by customer_id and status is taking 8 seconds. How do I figure out what's wrong?

evals.description: Core task: composite index design and column order
evals.prompt: I have a table with columns (id, org_id, status, created_at). Most queries filter by org_id and status, then sort by created_at descending. What index should I create and why does column order matter?
I need to add a NOT NULL column with a default to a 200 million row table in production without downtime. How should I approach this?

evals.description: Core task: reading an EXPLAIN plan and identifying a Seq Scan
evals.prompt: My EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows a Seq Scan on a table with 10 million rows even though I have an index on the column in my WHERE clause. Why is Postgres ignoring my index?
Should I use soft deletes or hard deletes for a user account deletion feature? We need to comply with GDPR right-to-erasure requests.
Our Node.js app opens a new Postgres connection per request and at peak load we're hitting 'too many connections'. What is the right fix?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns that OFFSET pagination degrades at scale
evals.prompt: We have a paginated list endpoint using LIMIT 20 OFFSET N. It works fine for page 1 but by page 5000 it takes 30 seconds. What's happening?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns that dropping a column requires expand-contract, not a direct ALTER
evals.prompt: I want to rename a column in a heavily used production table by dropping the old column and adding a new one in a single migration. Is this safe?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about replication lag on read replicas
evals.prompt: We're sending all reads after a write to a read replica to reduce load on primary. Users sometimes see their own write disappear. What's going on?

assertions.value: replicas always return the latest data

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend hash indexes as a general-purpose replacement for B-tree
evals.prompt: Someone told me hash indexes are faster than B-tree indexes. Should I switch all my indexes to hash?

evals.description: References load test: directs user to query-optimization reference for EXPLAIN deep dive
evals.prompt: Can you walk me through how to read a complex EXPLAIN ANALYZE output with multiple join nodes, hash batches, and buffer hits? I want to understand every line.
